results.title: Setapp is rated "Great" with 4.1 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
It feels like a frustrating case of bureaucracy over customer service. A company that once felt user-first now feels like it’s making arbitrary decisions at the expense of the very users who helped it grow.

Setapp, if you’re reading this: please do better. Give your loyal users the ability to make simple changes without losing the plans they’ve already earned.

May 2, 2025

Image 28: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## Used to love Setapp — but it’s really gone downhill
Lately, every time I start up my Mac, I get spammed by newsletter popups from apps like Hustl, CleanShot X, etc. Doesn’t matter how many times I click “No Thanks” — they keep coming back. Even tried clicking “Sure, I’m In” just to see if it would stop. Nope. Still getting hit daily.

Reached out to support, asked for a small courtesy credit after flagging the issue with screenshots. Denied. Gave them time to fix it. Still broken.

This feels spammy, careless, and honestly just lazy. Setapp used to be clean and worth it — now it’s starting to feel like bloatware with broken opt-outs and nonstop upsell noise. [...] But the real problem? The licensing system on iOS is beyond broken. Apps constantly check their license—multiple times per week. If you’re offline, you’re out of luck. No data? On a plane? In a dead zone? Too bad, you can’t use the apps you’re paying for. This is absurd. If a license check fails, give it three days before locking me out. This kind of aggressive DRM completely ruins the experience.

I love SetApp on macOS, but now I’m wondering if it has the same issue when offline. Unfortunately, SetApp is proving Apple right—so far, they are the only ones who can deliver a seamless mobile app experience. Disappointed.

February 18, 2025

results.title: Setapp is rated "Great" with 4.1 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Looking at 35 reviews, reviewers had a great experience with this company. Customers consistently praise the customer service for being helpful, responsive, and efficient in resolving issues. Many people appreciate the subscription model, finding it a great value for accessing a wide variety of useful applications. Reviewers highlight the extensive selection of apps, with new additions appearing r egularly, making it a valuable tool for their daily workflows. However, some customers also noted issues with billing inaccuracies and a perceived decline in the quality of customer service over time. A few other people also felt that the product's app selection was losing higher-value applications and that some included apps were broken or not working as expected.See more [...] While I do not use every app included, the ones I do use have become indispensable to my daily workflow. Whenever I have questions or run into issues, the support team is highly responsive and genuinely committed to finding solutions.

If Setapp includes apps you need, it is definitely worth taking a closer look.

One improvement I would love to see is a more active and structured forum where users can suggest new apps and vote on requests. I find the current Facebook-based solution rather difficult to navigate and somewhat unclear.

results.title: SetApp - who is still onboard? - Software - MPU Talk
results.content:
The whole drive to AI credits is a little annoying.

And most frustrating of all, the inclusion of “standalone” apps in SetApp. This is ridiculous, out of the 8 new arrivals apps, 7 of them are “standalone” and require additional monthly of once-off payment, only one app is included. This is completely against the whole idea of SetApp.

CleanShot 2026-05-30 at 16.10.49@2x

CleanShot 2026-05-30 at 16.10.49@2x934×64 5.07 KB

CleanShot 2026-05-30 at 16.10.49@2x

Update: I decided to try to explore a few more apps, just to be fair to SetApp. Control your notch looks interesting, nope it’s standalone. What about Proximate, nope. Or Deskspace, nope. Surely Wifilicious, nope. Or Diskmap, nope. MacPulse, nope. [...] I’m now more frustrated that my subscription rolled over, but that is absolutely my fault, and I won’t make that mistake 2027.

I left a long while back when they cranked up the monthly charge. There are some on here who seem to get value for money using apps like Noteplan.

It’s worth looking through all the apps and seeing what you could use, although this is a moot point if your dropping it in a year.

You can get a refund within 30 days, but you shouldn’t use any apps in the process. But even if you have it’s still worth making a refund request. [...] Other useful apps, but less important to me:  
Paste  
CleanMyMac  
iStat Menus  
AlDente Pro (Trying it out. Looks very promising.)  
Forecast Bar  
Pareto Security  
Mission Control Plus  
Moonlock  
Time Out  
Archiver  
Downie  
BetterZip  
PDF Squeezer  
SnapMotion

So it’s probably worth the money for me. Also, RichH’s point about one-offs is a good one, though I admit I haven’t used Setapp that way very much. Also, these are vetted apps, so probably safer than hunting around the Internet to find a solution.

Havining said all of that, the problems (standalone, tardy updates, etc) that SteveMac enumerates suggest to me a Setapp downhill corporate slide. I hope that isn’t the situation.

I always cancel 10 seconds after taking out the trial or subscription. That way I never forget.

results.title: Fixing permissions errors in Setapp – Setapp Support
results.content:
Logo

Sign in

 Setapp is broken after I moved some folders
 I’m an app developer. How can I join Setapp?

# Fixing permissions errors in Setapp

Sometimes, you might encounter problems when installing apps in Setapp. The installation may fail, and an accessibility error message appears:

> could not be installed due to a possible user permissions error.  
> Please make sure you resolve any related issues and try again.

Or it may look like this:

### Why this happens [...] Or it may look like this:

### Why this happens

Setapp installs apps in the /Applications/Setapp folder. If your user account doesn’t have Read & Write permissions for this folder, Setapp won’t be able to install or update apps properly.

### How to fix it

1. Open Finder > Applications.
2. Right-click the Setapp folder (not the app file) and choose Get Info.
3. In the Sharing & Permissions section, set your account to Read & Write.

   Tips:

    If the Sharing & Permissions section isn’t visible, click the disclosure triangle to expand it.
    If your account isn’t listed in the Name column, click the + button, choose your account, then click Select.

For step-by-step instructions, go to Apple’s guide on Change permissions for files, folders, or disks on Mac.

results.title: Setapp Review: Is It Worth It for Entrepreneurs?
results.content: flexibility is huge. And finally, updates are included, so you don't need to buy the new version of an app or worry about a surprise upgrade charge. As long as you're subscribed, you always have the latest version. That might sound small, but if you've ever been stuck with outdated software or forced to pay again just to get basic updates, you know how frustrating that can be. To make this more concrete, let's talk about a few apps that are included in Setup and why they matter for entrepreneurs. The first app is Clean My Mac. This helps keep your Mac fast and free of clutter. If your computer is your main office, the last thing you want is for it to be slowed down when you're on a deadline. This app scans your systems, removes junk files, frees up memory, and make sure everything runs [...] feature is the quality. Setup doesn't just throw in every random app. Each one is curated and maintained by its developers, so you're not wasting time testing broken or lowquality software. This gives you confidence that the apps you install will actually be reliable. The integration across Macs and iPhone is another highlight. Many of the apps are available on both platforms, which means you can work on your computer during the day and continue on your phone if you need to. I will say if you do want it on your phone as well, there is an extra cost there. So, just something to be mindful of, but we'll go through cost here towards the end of the video. For entrepreneurs who are constantly moving between home, office, and travel, that flexibility is huge. And finally, updates are included, [...] use a handful. That means you're technically paying for more than you actually need. The second con is overlap. If you already own some of the apps included, you may feel like you're double paying. And the third is platform limitation. though setup is Mac and iOS focused. If you are a Windows user, it's just not going to be a fit. For many entrepreneurs though, myself included, the pros outweigh the cons immensely. But it's still important to think about your own situation before committing and subscribing. So, who should actually consider SetApp? If you're a solo entrepreneur, a freelancer, SetApp is such a smart way to build a tool without spending too much money upfront.
